content.publication_details: This article has been copublished in Circulation. Copies are available on the websites of the American College of Cardiology and the American Heart Association (professional.heart.org). Permissions are limited and request can be made via the Elsevier site.

content.Aim: The 2024 ACC/AHA/AACVPR/APMA/ABC/SCAI/SVM/SVN/SVS/SIR/VESS Guideline for the Management of Lower Extremity Peripheral Artery Disease provides recommendations to guide clinicians in the treatment of patients with lower extremity peripheral artery disease across its multiple clinical presentation subsets (asymptomatic, chronic symptomatic, chronic limb-threatening ischemia, and acute limb ischemia).
content.Methods: A comprehensive literature search was conducted from October 2020 to June 2022, including studies, reviews, and other evidence on human subjects published in English from PubMed, EMBASE, the Cochrane Library, CINAHL Complete, and other selected databases. Additional studies published through May 2023 during the peer review process were considered and added to the evidence tables.
content.Structure: Recommendations from the 2016 AHA/ACC Guideline on the Management of Patients With Lower Extremity Peripheral Artery Disease have been updated with new evidence to guide clinicians, with new recommendations for comprehensive care for patients with peripheral artery disease.

content.Peripheral artery disease (PAD): A common cardiovascular disease associated with increased risk of amputation, myocardial infarction, stroke, and death, as well as impaired quality of life (QOL), walking performance, and functional status.
content.Detection: Through history, physical examination, and resting ankle-brachial index.
content.Health Disparities: Associated with poor limb and cardiovascular outcomes, to be addressed at individual and population levels.
content.Medical Therapy: Antiplatelet, antithrombotic therapy, lipid-lowering, antihypertensive therapy, management of diabetes, and smoking cessation. Rivaroxaban with aspirin to prevent major events and limb loss.
content.Structured Exercise: Core component for PAD care. Includes supervised exercise and community-based programs.
content.Revascularization: Used for chronic limb-threatening ischemia to improve QOL and prevent limb loss.
content.Multispecialty Care: Optimizes care for those with chronic limb-threatening ischemia.
content.Foot Care: Crucial across all clinical subsets and essential for chronic limb-threatening ischemia.
content.National Action Plan: Outlines strategic goals for improving awareness, detection, and treatment of PAD.

content.focus: Medical practice related to cardiovascular disease (CVD) with applicability in the US and globally.
content.intent: Improve quality of care and align with patients' interests.
content.clinical_implementation: Recommends management of disease through shared decision-making with an emphasis on patient engagement.
content.methodology: Regular review and update of guidelines by the AHA/ACC Joint Committee. Guidelines are modular with a focus on ease of access and cost-value considerations.
content.guideline_updates: Ongoing reviews and data analysis to keep guidelines current and relevant.
content.disclosure: Transparency with respect to relationships with industry and the development process.
content.evidence_review: Recommendations are based on evidence-based methodologies, systematic reviews, and expert opinion.

content.Management and Therapy: Guideline-directed management and therapy include clinical evaluation, diagnostic testing, and pharmacological and procedural treatments. Recommendations are based on evidence, and approved treatments are specified.
content.Methodology and Evidence Review: Extensive evidence-based literature review conducted from October 2020 to June 2022. Literature sourced from multiple databases including PubMed, EMBASE, Cochrane Library.
content.Scope: Lower extremity peripheral artery disease (PAD) is prevalent in 10 to 12 million individuals aged over 40 in the US, leading to significant health consequences.
